# Implementation Plan: Tags Filter

**Branch**: `001-tags-filter` | **Date**: 7. März 2026 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/001-tags-filter/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command.

## Summary

Introduce a `tags` field to all blog post markdown files (frontmatter) and add client-side tag filtering to the homepage. All posts are fetched at build time by the existing `getAllPosts()` Server Component. A two-column layout (`BlogWithSidebar` Client Component) renders the post list on the left and a sticky `TagCloud` sidebar on the right. The `TagCloud` displays all unique tags sorted by post frequency (most-used first), with font size scaled proportionally to frequency. Clicking a tag shows only posts carrying that tag; clicking the same tag or the "× Alle Beiträge" reset link resets the filter. Tags are normalized to lowercase at read time, deduplicated via `Set`. No new npm dependencies required.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript 5.x (strict mode)  
**Primary Dependencies**: Next.js 16.x, React 19.x, Tailwind CSS 4.x — no new dependencies added  
**Storage**: Markdown files in `lib/posts/` with YAML frontmatter (parsed by existing `gray-matter`)  
**Testing**: N/A — not requested in spec  
**Target Platform**: Web (static HTML/CSS/JS via `next build` / `output: export`)  
**Project Type**: Next.js App Router static site (personal blog)  
**Performance Goals**: Lighthouse Performance ≥90, Accessibility ≥95, Core Web Vitals pass (LCP <2.5s, CLS <0.1)  
**Constraints**: No new runtime dependencies; static-first (all posts fetched at build); client-side filtering on single URL `/`; TypeScript strict mode; no URL changes on filter interaction  
**Scale/Scope**: Personal blog (~10 posts), single homepage with tag filter overlay

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

**Required Checks** (based on my-blog constitution v1.0.0):

- [x] **Minimal Dependencies**: ✅ Zero new runtime dependencies. `gray-matter` (already installed) handles frontmatter; `useState`/`useMemo` are React built-ins.
- [x] **Component-First**: ✅ New `TagFilteredPostList` is a self-contained component in `components/TagFilteredPostList.tsx` with explicit TypeScript interfaces.
- [⚠️] **Static-First**: ⚠️ VIOLATION — `TagFilteredPostList` requires `"use client"` for `useState`. Justified: interactive filtering is the core feature requirement (FR-003, FR-004, FR-005). `app/page.tsx` stays a Server Component; only the list is a Client Component.
- [x] **Type Safety**: ✅ `BlogPost.tags: string[]` added to `lib/types.ts`. No `any` types. All component props explicitly typed.
- [x] **Accessibility & Performance**: ✅ `aria-pressed` on tag buttons, `<button>` elements, keyboard navigable, semantic HTML. CLS unaffected (tags rendered in static HTML, JS only toggles visibility). Lighthouse targets achievable.

**Result**: ⚠️ One justified violation — proceeding (see Complexity Tracking)

├── TagCloud.tsx          # NEW: "use client" — sticky sidebar tag cloud, frequency-sorted & -sized

lib/

**Structure Decision**: Next.js App Router (default for my-blog). No new routes, no API, no test files. `BlogWithSidebar` owns the `activeTag` state and renders a two-column grid (`grid-cols-[1fr_180px]` on desktop, single column on mobile). `TagCloud` is a pure presentational component receiving state and callbacks as props.

## Complexity Tracking

| Violation | Why Needed | Simpler Alternative Rejected Because |
|-----------|------------|-------------------------------------|
| `"use client"` on `TagFilteredPostList` | Interactive tag filtering requires `useState` (FR-003, FR-004, FR-005 — single-URL client-side filter) | Server Component cannot hold interactive state. Passing a `selectedTag` URL param would violate FR-005 (no URL changes). A pure CSS solution (`:target` pseudo-class) cannot filter a dynamically rendered list. Minimal bundle impact (~1–2 KB). |

**Justification approved**: Client Component is the minimal viable approach for stateful interactive filtering on a static site. `app/page.tsx` remains a Server Component; only the list sub-tree runs on the client.

---

## Phase 0: Research & Analysis (✅ COMPLETE)

**Objective**: Resolve all unknowns and establish technical approach.

**Deliverable**: [research.md](research.md)

**Key Findings**:
1. Server Component → Client Component boundary: pass `posts: BlogPost[]` as props — `Date` objects are supported by React RSC serialization, no type conversion needed.
2. Client-side filtering pattern: `useState<string | null>` for `activeTag`; `useMemo` for `allTags` (Set dedup + sort) and `filteredPosts`.
3. Tag normalization: `(data.tags ?? []).map((t: string) => t.toLowerCase().trim())` in `getAllPosts()` — runs once at build, not per render.
4. Accessibility: `<button aria-pressed={activeTag === tag}>` — WAI-ARIA 1.1 toggle button pattern.
5. Dedup + sort: `Array.from(new Set(posts.flatMap(p => p.tags))).sort()` — no library needed.

**Technologies Finalized**: Next.js 16.x App Router, React 19.x, TypeScript 5.x strict, Tailwind CSS 4.x. Zero new dependencies.

**Status**: ✅ All research questions answered.

---

## Phase 1: Design & Contracts (✅ COMPLETE)

**Objective**: Define data structures, component interfaces, and integration points.

**Deliverables**:
- ✅ [data-model.md](data-model.md) — entity definitions, normalization rules, filter state model, frontmatter schema
- ✅ [quickstart.md](quickstart.md) — developer implementation guide
- ℹ️ `contracts/` — N/A: no API endpoints, no external data contracts for this feature

**Key Design Decisions**:

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Filter mechanism | `useState<string \| null>` in Client Component | Static-compatible, no new deps |
| Component name | `TagFilteredPostList` | Self-describing, component-first |
| Data flow | `getAllPosts()` → Server Component → props → Client Component | RSC serialization supports `Date` |
| Tag normalization | `toLowerCase().trim()` at read time in `getAllPosts()` | Once per build, not per render |
| Tag dedup | `Set` | Built-in, zero cost |
| Tag sort | `Array.prototype.sort()` | Alphabetical, built-in |
| Active tag toggle | Click same tag again → deactivate OR "Alle Beiträge" button | Two clear affordances |
| Accessibility | `aria-pressed` on `<button>` | WAI-ARIA 1.1 standard |

**Files to Create**:
- `components/BlogWithSidebar.tsx` (new — "use client" — two-column layout + filter state)
- `components/TagCloud.tsx` (new — "use client" — sticky sidebar tag cloud)

**Files to Modify**:
- `lib/types.ts` — add `tags: string[]` to `BlogPost`
- `lib/posts.ts` — read and normalize tags in `getAllPosts()`
- `app/page.tsx` — replace `TagFilteredPostList` with `BlogWithSidebar`; widen to `max-w-5xl`
- `components/Navigation.tsx` — widen inner container to `max-w-5xl`
- `lib/posts/*.md` — add `tags:` frontmatter to all 7 posts

**Status**: ✅ Design complete. Ready for task execution.
